description: To fall: leaves from branches, rain from clouds, a cup from a hand. Downward motion surrendered to rather than made. Distinct from 'thenoi' (falls-short, of magnitudes) and from 'muero' (autumn, the season of falling).
sound_symbolism: The liquid 'l' lets go, glides down through 'e', and lands on the open 'a' — the release, the descent, the arrival at ground, in three sounds.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive: 'lirowa lepa.' (The leaf falls.) With past marking: 'soeli to lepa.' (The cup fell.) What falls is simply the subject; gravity needs no agent.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Falling is the body's plainest lesson in non-clinging: what is released descends, and resisting the descent changes nothing. The leaf's fall is the classic meditation image.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Rain falls, seed falls, fruit falls when ripe — agriculture is the craft of working with what falls and when.

semantic_domains.nature: What leaves, rain, and ripe fruit do
